title: Korthalsella aoraiensis

There is limited information on the plant Korthalsella aoraiensis. However, I can provide some general information about the genus Korthalsella. 

Korthalsella is a genus of flowering plants in the family Santalaceae, commonly known as mistletoes, but they are actually parasitic plants that live on other plants. 

Korthalsella aoraiensis is a species of mistletoe native to the Pacific Islands, however,  due to lack of data, the health benefits and bioactive constituents of this specific plant are unknown.
taxonomy: Korthalsella aoraiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Santalaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. The classification of Korthalsella aoraiensis includes its kingdom as Plantae and its phylum as Magnoliophyta. At the class level, it is classified as Magnoliopsida, and at the order level, it is classified as Santalales. This plant is endemic to the Aoraki region in New Zealand's South Island.
